food_subgroup: Crustaceans — whole crab dish in sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for Singapore Chilli Crab. FOODON_03302757 (crab dish) is the closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CJW covers crabs as a commodity; no prepared chilli crab dish code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Singapore Chilli Crab as a prepared dish.

preparation_method: whole crab cooked in chilli tomato egg-drop sauce

serving.description: 1 large whole crab with sauce (approximately 600–800 g edible portion per serving for a large crab)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of large Singapore Chilli Crab is approximately 400 g of crab meat plus sauce, representing roughly half of a large whole mud crab as served at a restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Chilli Crab — HPB Singapore Foodo / Health Promotion Board Nutrient Composition Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Chilli Crab nutrient values per 100 g edible portion. The large-size variant (辣椒螃蟹（大）) is the same dish at a larger serving size; per-100 g nutrient values are identical to the standard Chilli Crab entry. Values below are per 100 g edible portion including sauce as reported by HPB Foodo. Where HPB Foodo does not report a micronutrient, USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy, crab cooked moist heat, item 15139) was used as a secondary reference for micronutrient estimation, clearly noted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singapore Chilli Crab as a composite dish. GI estimated at 35 based on the dominant glycemic contributors: tomato-based sauce (GI ~35, low), egg (GI ~0, negligible), and crab meat (GI ~0, protein-dominant). The sauce contributes the primary carbohydrate load (sugar, cornstarch, tomato). A mixed seafood dish with tomato-based sauce analogous to prawn in tomato sauce (GI ~35) from the University of Sydney GI Database is used as the closest defensible analogous food. GL per serving calculated as (5.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 400 g serving / 100) × 35 / 100 = 7.84, rounded to 7.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of Chilli Crab due to its high sodium content from soy sauce and seasoning, with a single restaurant serving potentially providing 70–84% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load with added sugar in sauce; low GI but significant GL at restaurant serving sizes
type2_diabetes.guidance: Chilli Crab has a low glycemic index (~35) due to its protein and fat content, but the sauce contains added sugar and cornstarch; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and avoid accompanying fried mantou (deep-fried buns) to limit total glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate cholesterol; beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from crab meat partially offset risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Chilli Crab provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids and lean protein from crab meat, but the high sodium content and moderate cholesterol (~72 mg per 100 g) warrant portion control for individuals with cardiovascular disease, particularly those managing blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Singapore Chilli Crab (辣椒螃蟹) is one of Singapore's most iconic national dishes, typically prepared using whole mud crab (Scylla serrata) stir-fried and simmered in a rich, semi-thick sauce made from blended chillies, tomato sauce, egg, garlic, ginger, shallots, soy sauce, sugar, and cornstarch. The sauce is savoury, mildly spicy, and slightly sweet with a characteristic egg-drop texture from beaten eggs stirred in at the end of cooking. The crab shell turns bright red during cooking and the dish is traditionally served whole at the table, accompanied by deep-fried or steamed mantou buns used to soak up the sauce. The large variant (大) refers to a larger whole crab, typically 800 g to 1.2 kg live weight, commanding a premium price. Nutritionally, Chilli Crab is a moderate-calorie dish (~105 kcal per 100 g) with a good protein content (~10 g per 100 g) from the crab meat, moderate fat (~4.8 g), and a relatively low carbohydrate content (~5.6 g) primarily from the sauce. It is a meaningful source of vitamin B12, zinc, selenium, and phosphorus from the crab. The dish is high in sodium (~420 mg per 100 g) due to soy sauce and seasoning, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low), though the sauce contains added sugar. Chilli Crab is not halal-certified in most restaurants due to cross-contamination risk, and contains crustacean shellfish, egg, and soy allergens. It is a centrepiece dish for celebratory meals and a defining element of Singaporean culinary identity.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Chilli Crab?
What are the allergens in Chilli Crab?
Is Chilli Crab high in sodium?
What is the difference between Chilli Crab and Black Pepper Crab?
How is Singapore Chilli Crab made?

confidence_reason: HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes and lists Chilli Crab nutrient data. The large-size variant shares the same per-100 g nutritional profile as the standard dish. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct GI measurement for this composite dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Micronutrients not reported by HPB Foodo are supplemented from USDA SR Legacy crab data. Overall confidence is medium due to the GI estimation and the composite nature of the dish.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Fish and seafood' — the dish is a crab-based seafood preparation, not a grain or staple. 2. Cuisine corrected from generic 'Chinese' to 'Singaporean' — Chilli Crab is a Singaporean national dish, not a mainland Chinese dish. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this dish. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source database for this dish. 5. Trans fat is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for this dish type per HPB Foodo; naturally zero for a fresh seafood and vegetable-based sauce preparation without hydrogenated fats. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (35, low) based on the closest defensible analogous food (seafood in tomato-based sauce) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for Singapore Chilli Crab. 7. The dish is not halal-certified at most Singapore restaurants due to the use of non-halal crab sourcing and kitchen cross-contamination; is_halal is set to false. 8. The large (大) size designation refers to serving/crab size only and does not alter per-100 g nutrient values. 9. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
